governanceControlObjective: Maintain a complete inventory of AI systems, models, agents, tools, owners and deployments.
minimumImplementation: Register every AI system before production and assign accountable business and technical owners.
requiredEvidence: AI register; asset ID; owner; purpose; model; data; tools; risk status; deployment locations.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog securables, Catalog Explorer, model/MCP services and MLflow registry.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry Control Plane Assets and Admin panes.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Model Registry/Dashboard, Bedrock resources, tags, AWS Config and asset inventory patterns.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Registry, Model Registry, Cloud Asset Inventory and Gemini Enterprise Admin.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Governance, technical documentation and accountability; GDPR accountability.
isoIec42001Theme: Context, leadership, planning and documented information.
importantNote: Inventory must include shadow AI and externally hosted agents/models, not only provider-native resources.

governanceControlObjective: Define who approves, builds, operates, monitors, changes and retires each AI system.
minimumImplementation: Use a RACI and named risk acceptance authority by use-case risk tier.
requiredEvidence: RACI; approval authority; escalation path; system owner acceptance.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog owners, grants and workspace/account administrator roles.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry project roles, Entra groups and Control Plane Admin.
awsImplementation: IAM roles, SageMaker Role Manager, Organizations and resource ownership tags.
googleCloudImplementation: IAM roles, Agent Identity, organization/folder/project ownership.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Accountability and human oversight; GDPR accountability.
importantNote: A cloud administrator is not automatically the accountable AI-system owner.

governanceControlObjective: Classify AI use cases by impact, autonomy, users, data sensitivity and regulatory context.
minimumImplementation: Use a documented risk taxonomy before model or platform selection.
requiredEvidence: Risk assessment; impact level; prohibited/high-risk screening; rationale.
databricksImplementation: Tags/classifications in Unity Catalog and governance workflow outside the platform.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry compliance metadata, Purview classifications and GRC workflow.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Model Cards risk rating, Audit Manager and GRC workflow.
googleCloudImplementation: Labels/metadata, governance policies and external GRC workflow.
euAiActGdprRelevance: AI Act risk classification and GDPR risk-based accountability.
isoIec42001Theme: Risk assessment and AI system impact assessment.
importantNote: Provider controls do not determine whether a use case is legally high risk.

governanceControlObjective: Prevent deployment of use cases that breach organizational policy or applicable law.
minimumImplementation: Screen at intake and before material changes; block prohibited categories.
requiredEvidence: Signed screening checklist; policy outcome; exception record.
databricksImplementation: Service policies and catalog access can enforce technical blocks after policy decision.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry Compliance guardrail policies and Azure Policy.
awsImplementation: Organizations SCPs, IAM, Service Catalog restrictions and Guardrails.
googleCloudImplementation: Organization Policy, semantic governance and access controls.
euAiActGdprRelevance: AI Act prohibited-practice screening; GDPR fairness/lawfulness.
importantNote: This control requires legal interpretation and cannot be automated solely by the cloud platform.

governanceControlObjective: Ensure staff understand AI capabilities, limitations, risks and their assigned responsibilities.
minimumImplementation: Role-based training for executives, product owners, engineers, validators, operators and users.
requiredEvidence: Training curriculum; attendance; competency records; refresh schedule.
databricksImplementation: Databricks training plus organization policy and role enablement.
microsoftImplementation: Microsoft Learn/organizational training linked to Foundry roles.
awsImplementation: AWS Skill Builder/organizational training linked to IAM roles.
googleCloudImplementation: Google Cloud Skills Boost/organizational training linked to IAM roles.
euAiActGdprRelevance: AI literacy obligations; GDPR staff awareness.
importantNote: Training must cover the actual system, not only generic prompt-writing.

governanceControlObjective: Trace training, tuning, grounding and evaluation data to source and permitted use.
minimumImplementation: Record source, owner, collection basis, transformations, versions and downstream use.
requiredEvidence: Data lineage; dataset card; source contracts; licence and consent records.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog lineage, tables/volumes, tags and MLflow run/model lineage.
microsoftImplementation: Microsoft Purview lineage, Azure ML lineage and data assets.
awsImplementation: Glue/DataZone/Lake Formation lineage, SageMaker lineage and S3 inventory.
googleCloudImplementation: Dataplex lineage, ML Metadata, BigQuery/Cloud Storage metadata.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Data governance and technical documentation; GDPR purpose limitation and lawfulness.
isoIec42001Theme: Data for AI systems and impact assessment.
importantNote: A model-provider training-data statement does not replace lineage for enterprise grounding data.

governanceControlObjective: Demonstrate that data is sufficiently accurate, complete, relevant and representative for the use case.
minimumImplementation: Define data-quality rules, population coverage and known limitations.
requiredEvidence: Data-quality report; sampling analysis; bias analysis; remediation log.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog metadata, data-quality tooling and MLflow evaluation datasets.
microsoftImplementation: Purview Data Quality, Fabric/Databricks, Azure ML data validation.
awsImplementation: Glue Data Quality, SageMaker Data Wrangler/Clarify and Model Monitor.
googleCloudImplementation: Dataplex/Knowledge Catalog data quality, Agent Platform evaluation.
euAiActGdprRelevance: High-risk data governance; GDPR accuracy.
isoIec42001Theme: Data quality, impact assessment and operational planning.
importantNote: RAG document quality and model-training-data quality are different assessments.

governanceControlObjective: Assess privacy risks where personal data is used in prompts, training, retrieval, logs or decisions.
minimumImplementation: Perform DPIA or equivalent risk assessment when required; identify lawful basis and mitigations.
requiredEvidence: DPIA; data-flow diagram; lawful-basis decision; privacy controls; consultation record.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog classifications, access controls, logging and external privacy workflow.
microsoftImplementation: Microsoft Purview/Priva, Foundry controls and Azure privacy/security configuration.
awsImplementation: Macie, Audit Manager, Lake Formation and organizational privacy workflow.
googleCloudImplementation: Sensitive Data Protection, Cloud DLP, IAM and privacy workflow.
euAiActGdprRelevance: GDPR DPIA, lawfulness, fairness and transparency; AI Act privacy considerations.
importantNote: Cloud certification does not remove the deployer's GDPR responsibilities.

governanceControlObjective: Limit data collected, retrieved, logged and retained to what the AI task needs.
minimumImplementation: Minimise context, tool outputs, logs, memory and copied data fields.
requiredEvidence: Field-level data map; redaction rules; retrieval filters; logging configuration.
databricksImplementation: Row/column controls, views, service policies and selective inference logging.
microsoftImplementation: Purview, DLP, Prompt Shields/Content Safety and logging configuration.
awsImplementation: Macie, Lake Formation, Bedrock Guardrails PII filters and logging controls.
googleCloudImplementation: Sensitive Data Protection, Model Armor, RAG filters and logging configuration.
euAiActGdprRelevance: GDPR data minimisation and purpose limitation.
importantNote: More context can improve quality but increases privacy, cost and prompt-injection exposure.

governanceControlObjective: Define how long prompts, responses, traces, embeddings, sessions and learned memory persist.
minimumImplementation: Set retention periods, deletion workflows, legal holds and user correction/deletion mechanisms.
requiredEvidence: Retention schedule; TTL/lifecycle configuration; deletion test; exception register.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog Delta retention/lifecycle, system-table constraints and application deletion workflows.
microsoftImplementation: Azure Monitor/storage retention, Foundry data settings and application deletion workflows.
awsImplementation: S3/CloudWatch lifecycle, Bedrock/SageMaker logging settings and application deletion workflows.
googleCloudImplementation: Zero-data-retention settings, BigQuery TTL, Logging retention and Memory Bank controls.
euAiActGdprRelevance: GDPR storage limitation and rights; AI Act record-keeping interactions.
importantNote: Deleting an application record might not delete derived embeddings, caches or model memory unless designed explicitly.

governanceControlObjective: Restrict humans and workloads to the minimum AI, data and administrative privileges.
minimumImplementation: Separate builder, validator, approver, operator and auditor roles.
requiredEvidence: Access matrix; role definitions; access review; privileged-access evidence.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog privileges, ABAC, workspace bindings and admin roles.
microsoftImplementation: Entra ID, Azure RBAC, Conditional Access and Foundry roles.
awsImplementation: IAM, SageMaker Role Manager, Organizations and permission boundaries.
googleCloudImplementation: IAM, Agent Identity, service accounts and Access Context Manager.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Cybersecurity and access controls; GDPR integrity/confidentiality.
importantNote: Model invocation, tool execution and data access are separate permissions.

governanceControlObjective: Assign non-human identities and control delegation to downstream tools.
minimumImplementation: Use managed identities; prohibit shared API keys; document on-behalf-of flows.
requiredEvidence: Identity diagram; service account/role; token scope; rotation and delegation tests.
databricksImplementation: Unified authentication, service principals and Unity Catalog authorization.
microsoftImplementation: Microsoft Entra agent identity and managed identity.
awsImplementation: IAM roles for Bedrock/SageMaker agents and workload federation.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Identity, service accounts and Workload Identity Federation.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Cybersecurity, human oversight and accountability.
importantNote: Agent identity must persist through tool calls so actions remain attributable.

governanceControlObjective: Prevent agents from invoking unauthorized tools, functions, records or actions.
minimumImplementation: Allowlist tools, filter operations, enforce resource-level permissions and transaction limits.
requiredEvidence: Tool inventory; permission map; negative tests; approval thresholds.
databricksImplementation: MCP Services, tool filtering, functions, connections and service policies.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry tools, Entra permissions, API Management and workflow approvals.
awsImplementation: Bedrock action groups, IAM, Lambda resource policies and Verified Permissions.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Gateway, Agent Registry, IAM and semantic governance policies.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Human oversight and cybersecurity; GDPR access limitation.
importantNote: A safe model can still cause harm through an over-privileged tool.

governanceControlObjective: Select models using quality, risk, privacy, licensing, region and support evidence.
minimumImplementation: Compare candidate models on representative tasks and complete provider due diligence.
requiredEvidence: Model evaluation; provider terms; data-use review; licence; region; exit plan.
databricksImplementation: Model services can expose Databricks-hosted and external-provider models under common governance.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry Models benchmark, model catalog and provider documentation.
awsImplementation: SageMaker JumpStart and AWS Marketplace evaluation; Bedrock model catalog.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Platform models, benchmark/evaluation and provider/model cards.
euAiActGdprRelevance: GPAI/provider obligations and deployer due diligence; GDPR processor/vendor governance.
importantNote: The highest benchmark model is not automatically the lowest-risk or lowest-TCO model.

governanceControlObjective: Document intended use, limitations, architecture, data, model, prompts, tools and owners.
minimumImplementation: Maintain model cards plus an end-to-end AI system card.
requiredEvidence: System card; model card; architecture; limitations; risk rating; approvals.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog/MLflow metadata, tags, descriptions, lineage and registered models.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry/Azure ML metadata, Purview and Responsible AI artifacts.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Model Cards and Model Registry; Bedrock provider model cards plus customer-maintained system documentation.
googleCloudImplementation: Model Registry metadata, labels and customer-maintained system card.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Technical documentation, transparency and record keeping.
importantNote: The system card must cover orchestration and tools, not only the foundation model.

governanceControlObjective: Prove that quality, safety, security and operational thresholds are met before release.
minimumImplementation: Use a versioned evaluation dataset, pass/fail thresholds and independent review for higher-risk systems.
requiredEvidence: Evaluation plan; dataset; results; defects; approval and residual risk.
databricksImplementation: MLflow 3 evaluation, traces, scorers and human feedback.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry evaluations, agent evaluators and red-team results.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Clarify/Experiments/custom eval; Bedrock Evaluations.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Platform Model/Agent Evaluation, offline and simulated evaluation.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity, risk management and conformity evidence.
isoIec42001Theme: Performance evaluation, monitoring and risk treatment.
importantNote: Average scores can conceal severe subgroup or edge-case failures.

governanceControlObjective: Assess whether outcomes are unjustifiably different and provide appropriate explanations.
minimumImplementation: Define affected groups, fairness metrics and explanation needs based on the decision context.
requiredEvidence: Bias/fairness report; explanation design; user testing; limitations.
databricksImplementation: MLflow/custom analysis with governed artifacts.
microsoftImplementation: Azure ML Responsible AI dashboard and explainability.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Clarify.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Platform Explainable AI and custom fairness analysis.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Fairness, non-discrimination, transparency and GDPR fairness.
isoIec42001Theme: Impact assessment, transparency and responsible use.
importantNote: LLM explanation text is not necessarily a faithful explanation of model reasoning.

governanceControlObjective: Detect and control harmful or disallowed input and output content.
minimumImplementation: Apply domain-specific policies, thresholds, testing and exception handling.
requiredEvidence: Guardrail configuration; test set; blocked/allowed examples; override process.
databricksImplementation: Unity AI Gateway service policies and guardrail patterns.
microsoftImplementation: Azure AI Content Safety and Foundry guardrail policies.
awsImplementation: Bedrock Guardrails; SageMaker/custom classifiers where needed.
googleCloudImplementation: Model Armor and Agent Platform safety controls.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Risk management, cybersecurity and transparency.
importantNote: Generic toxicity filters do not cover every sector-specific policy or harm.

governanceControlObjective: Reduce direct and indirect attempts to override instructions or exfiltrate data.
minimumImplementation: Use layered controls: input screening, retrieval filtering, least privilege, tool authorization and output validation.
requiredEvidence: Threat model; adversarial tests; prompt-attack metrics; mitigation evidence.
lifecycleGate: Security test gate and continuous monitoring
databricksImplementation: Service policies, tool filtering, access controls and custom policies.
microsoftImplementation: Prompt Shields, red teaming, agent evaluation and Defender integration.
awsImplementation: Bedrock Guardrails prompt filters, IAM, WAF and application controls.
googleCloudImplementation: Model Armor, Semantic Governance Policy, Agent Gateway and SCC AI Protection.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Cybersecurity, robustness and data protection.
importantNote: Prompt filters alone cannot secure an agent with excessive tool privileges.

governanceControlObjective: Prevent models and agents from exposing confidential or personal data.
minimumImplementation: Classify data, enforce ACLs, minimise context, inspect outputs and protect logs.
requiredEvidence: DLP policy; data-access tests; redaction tests; leakage evaluation.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog access controls, masks, service policies and governed logging.
microsoftImplementation: Purview DLP, Content Safety, Defender, Azure AI Search ACLs.
awsImplementation: Macie, Lake Formation, Bedrock Guardrails sensitive-information filters.
googleCloudImplementation: Sensitive Data Protection, Model Armor, VPC Service Controls.
euAiActGdprRelevance: GDPR security, confidentiality and minimisation; AI Act cybersecurity.
importantNote: RAG can leak data even when the base model never stores customer prompts.

governanceControlObjective: Test abuse paths, adversarial prompts, harmful autonomy and unexpected tool combinations.
minimumImplementation: Create threat-led scenarios and retest after model, prompt, tool or policy changes.
requiredEvidence: Red-team plan; findings; severity; remediation; retest results.
databricksImplementation: MLflow/custom adversarial evaluations and logged traces.
microsoftImplementation: AI Red Teaming Agent and scheduled scans.
awsImplementation: Bedrock/SageMaker custom test harness plus AWS security testing.
googleCloudImplementation: Model Armor, SCC AI Protection and custom adversarial evaluation.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Cybersecurity, robustness and post-market risk management.
isoIec42001Theme: Testing, corrective action and continual improvement.
importantNote: Red teaming must include business-process abuse, not only unsafe text generation.

governanceControlObjective: Ensure people can understand, intervene, override or challenge consequential AI outcomes.
minimumImplementation: Define oversight role, intervention point, escalation, appeal and fallback process.
requiredEvidence: Human-oversight SOP; decision thresholds; override logs; appeal mechanism.
databricksImplementation: Application workflow, service-policy approvals and audit logs.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry/Power Platform/Logic Apps workflows and operational controls.
awsImplementation: A2I, Step Functions, Connect and application approvals.
googleCloudImplementation: Workflows, application approval patterns and Agent Gateway policies.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Human oversight, transparency and rights relating to automated decisions.
isoIec42001Theme: Human oversight, communication and operational control.
importantNote: A nominal human in the loop is ineffective if they lack time, information or authority.

governanceControlObjective: Prevent untested model, prompt, RAG, agent or policy changes from reaching production.
minimumImplementation: Version all material artifacts; require testing, approval, rollback and separation of duties.
requiredEvidence: Change record; test results; approval; deployment ID; rollback test.
databricksImplementation: MLflow Registry, deployment jobs, Unity Catalog tags and CI/CD.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry projects, Azure ML registry, GitHub/Azure DevOps and policies.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Registry/Pipelines, CodePipeline and infrastructure as code.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Platform Registry/Pipelines, Cloud Build/Deploy and IaC.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Quality-management and record-keeping requirements.
isoIec42001Theme: Change management, operational control and documented information.
importantNote: A foundation-model version change can be a material system change even when application code is unchanged.

governanceControlObjective: Capture security, administrative, data-access and AI-runtime events.
minimumImplementation: Define required events, retention, access, integrity and correlation IDs.
requiredEvidence: Logging standard; sample logs; retention; access review; integrity evidence.
databricksImplementation: Audit logs, system tables, inference tables and Unity Catalog lineage.
microsoftImplementation: Azure Activity/Entra logs, Foundry traces, Purview and Defender alerts.
awsImplementation: CloudTrail, CloudWatch, SageMaker data capture and Bedrock invocation logging.
googleCloudImplementation: Cloud Audit Logs, Agent Observability and request-response logs.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Record keeping, cybersecurity and GDPR accountability/security.
importantNote: Prompt/response logs can themselves become a sensitive regulated dataset.

governanceControlObjective: Trace retrieval, model, agent, tool and downstream-system calls.
minimumImplementation: Use a shared trace/correlation ID and preserve enough context to diagnose failures.
requiredEvidence: Trace sample; topology; latency breakdown; tool-call records.
databricksImplementation: MLflow Tracing and inference tables.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry/OpenTelemetry/Application Insights tracing.
awsImplementation: X-Ray/OpenTelemetry, SageMaker and Bedrock traces.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Observability traces and topology with Cloud Trace.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Technical documentation, record keeping and incident investigation.
importantNote: Do not log sensitive payloads simply to improve traceability.

governanceControlObjective: Detect degradation, unsafe behaviour or policy violations in production.
minimumImplementation: Define AI-specific SLIs, thresholds, sampling and escalation.
requiredEvidence: Monitoring dashboard; alerts; quality trends; review minutes.
databricksImplementation: MLflow monitoring, inference tables and system tables.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry continuous evaluation and monitoring dashboards.
awsImplementation: Model Monitor, CloudWatch and scheduled Bedrock evaluations.
googleCloudImplementation: Online monitoring, Agent Observability and Cloud Monitoring.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Post-market monitoring, risk management and incident detection.
importantNote: Availability can be healthy while answer quality is failing.

governanceControlObjective: Detect changes in data, users, retrieval, models, prompts, tools or outcomes.
minimumImplementation: Baseline relevant distributions and define review triggers.
requiredEvidence: Drift report; thresholds; root-cause analysis; remediation.
databricksImplementation: Lakehouse Monitoring, MLflow metrics and inference data.
microsoftImplementation: Azure ML monitoring and Foundry quality/risk trends.
awsImplementation: SageMaker Model Monitor and CloudWatch.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Platform Model Monitoring and online evaluation.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Accuracy/robustness and post-market monitoring.
importantNote: For agents, tool and workflow drift can be more important than statistical feature drift.

governanceControlObjective: Respond to harmful output, data leakage, unauthorized action or material model failure.
minimumImplementation: Define severity, containment, rollback, notification, investigation and corrective action.
requiredEvidence: AI incident playbook; exercise results; incident log; root cause and corrective action.
databricksImplementation: Disable/revoke privileges, service-policy changes, audit/inference tables and rollback.
microsoftImplementation: Control Plane remediation, Defender/Purview alerts and deployment rollback.
awsImplementation: IAM revocation, Guardrails updates, endpoint rollback, Security Hub and incident tooling.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Gateway/policy changes, IAM revocation, SCC and deployment rollback.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Serious-incident and post-market obligations may apply; GDPR breach process may apply.
isoIec42001Theme: Incident management, nonconformity and corrective action.
importantNote: Legal notification thresholds and timelines require specialist review.

governanceControlObjective: Prevent runaway agents, excessive token use and resource exhaustion.
minimumImplementation: Set budgets, rate limits, loop limits, timeouts, quotas and anomaly alerts.
requiredEvidence: Budget; limits; alert; load test; abuse scenarios; monthly cost review.
databricksImplementation: Unity AI Gateway budgets, hard caps, rate limits and usage tables.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry token limits, quota pane, Azure budgets and cost monitoring.
awsImplementation: AWS Budgets, Service Quotas, API limits, SageMaker/Bedrock metrics.
googleCloudImplementation: Agent Gateway, Cloud Quotas, budgets and billing export.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Operational resilience and governance; indirect compliance impact.
importantNote: Agent loops and long context can create both financial and denial-of-service risk.

governanceControlObjective: Maintain versioned AI policies and continuously assess adherence.
minimumImplementation: Attach controls to assets, track assignments, exceptions, review dates and remediation.
requiredEvidence: Policy version; assignment list; exception; compliance dashboard; remediation evidence.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog policies/privileges, service-policy attachments and system tables.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry Compliance pane, Azure Policy, Purview and Defender.
awsImplementation: AWS Config, Audit Manager, Organizations, IAM and Guardrail versions.
googleCloudImplementation: Organization Policy, IAM governance, Agent Gateway and SCC.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Governance, risk management and technical documentation.
isoIec42001Theme: Policy, control, audit and continual improvement.
importantNote: A framework mapping is evidence of intent, not proof that the control operates effectively.

governanceControlObjective: Provide reproducible evidence that controls operated as designed.
minimumImplementation: Preserve immutable-enough records, independent review and traceable samples.
requiredEvidence: Audit pack; evidence index; test samples; findings; management response.
databricksImplementation: Unity Catalog audit/system tables, lineage, MLflow and policy records.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry/Purview/Defender evidence, Azure logs and policy compliance.
awsImplementation: Audit Manager, CloudTrail, Config, Model Cards and monitoring evidence.
googleCloudImplementation: Cloud Audit Logs, SCC, Assured Workloads and platform evidence.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Conformity assessment and accountability; GDPR accountability.
isoIec42001Theme: Internal audit, management review and documented information.
importantNote: Cloud-provider certifications cover provider controls, not the customer's AI system design.

governanceControlObjective: Tell users when they interact with AI and provide meaningful limitations and escalation information.
minimumImplementation: Create context-specific notices, AI-generated-content marking where applicable and support routes.
requiredEvidence: User notice; interface screenshots; content marking tests; complaint/escalation process.
databricksImplementation: Application-level responsibility; metadata/logs can prove the deployed version.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry/application controls and Microsoft content credentials/labeling where supported.
awsImplementation: Application-level notices; Bedrock/SageMaker logs and model documentation.
googleCloudImplementation: Application-level notices and Google model/content provenance features where supported.
euAiActGdprRelevance: AI Act transparency obligations and GDPR transparency.
importantNote: Transparency requirements depend on role, use case and content; obtain legal review.

governanceControlObjective: Review real-world performance, incidents, complaints and material changes after deployment.
minimumImplementation: Define monitoring plan, review cadence, triggers and responsible committee.
requiredEvidence: Post-market plan; monthly/quarterly reviews; complaint trends; change decisions.
databricksImplementation: Inference tables, MLflow monitoring, audit logs and governance reviews.
microsoftImplementation: Foundry monitoring/compliance dashboards and Control Plane reviews.
awsImplementation: Model Monitor, CloudWatch, Guardrails metrics and governance review.
googleCloudImplementation: Online monitoring, Agent Observability, SCC and governance review.
euAiActGdprRelevance: Post-market monitoring and risk-management duties may apply.
isoIec42001Theme: Monitoring, management review and continual improvement.
importantNote: A system that passed pre-deployment testing can become unacceptable as context or usage changes.
